RED DEL VINO

VITICULTURAL
WINE TOUR

[vc_column_text]Spend a fun the day learning about the winemaking process in Colchagua. As part of the program, you will visit 3 private vineyards and get to know the winemakers who have a long history in the area. They’ll share their secrets and techniques in harvesting grapes and walk you through the production process.

The vineyards are located in Patagua, Chépica, Apalta, Palmilla, Nancagua y Peralillo.

Includes:

  • A private visit to 1 vineyard in Colchagua
  • Spanish tour guide
  • Tasting of 2 wines

Not included:

  • Transportation

[/vc_column_text]

[/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner width=”1/2″]
[vc_column_text]Duration: Approximately 1 hour
Tour Hours: This tour is available between 10:00 am and 6:00 pm daily[/vc_column_text]
Cost per person:
– $10.000 pesos
– Payment due at beginning of tour.
[/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row full_width=”stretch_row_content”][vc_column]

CAMPESINO CHARDONNAY
GRAN RESERVA

[vc_column_text]View: The appearance of this wine is a very clear and clean yellow tone.

Nose: Initially mineral with notes of almond, hazelnut and melon, citrus notes lime / lemon, and peach.

Mouth: Moderate acidity, medium structure and short finish.

Pairing: Ideal for seafood and smoked fish, pasta dishes with not very spicy white sauce and vegan food.

Serving temperature: 12 °[/vc_column_text]

[/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner width=”1/2″]
RED DEL VINO

Campesino Carménère

[vc_column_text]View: Clean wine without sediment and bright (young), has a high layer, deep ruby red color.

Smells: Fruit aromas such as plum and cherries, its aging in barrels provides notes such as chocolate and vanilla, leaving a pleasant aroma before tasting In mouth: This wine has a good structure in the mouth, its tannins are balanced and very rich in acidity.

Pairing: Ideal to accompany red meats of beef, and game meats of intense flavor. It also goes well with mature and penetrating cheeses such as Parmesan, Gruyere and Camembert.

Serving temperature: 16 ° and 18 °[/vc_column_text]

RED DEL VINO

CAMPESINO CHARDONNAY

[vc_column_text]View: The appearance of this wine is a very clear and clean yellow tone.

Smell: The first impression on the nose is very fruity, mainly to mangoes, ripe tropical fruits and evolves towards notes of citrus and spices such as cinnamon. In the mouth: In the mouth it has a medium, soft and rich body, which ends with a vibrant acidity that gives it a medium length and palate. The aftertaste evolves towards more oak nuances, like vanilla and toasted.

Pairing: Perfect to accompany delicate fish such as croaker, sole and turbot, grilled with creamy sauces or capers or herbs

Serving temperature: 12 °[/vc_column_text]

RED DEL VINO

JOSÉ LUIS CÁCERES

[vc_column_text]José Luis y Ángel are brothers and also founding partners of Red del Vino.

They live in the former San José del Carmen Estate which is a historic site and adds great value to their land.

Their father worked in property management and with time transitioned to work in the fields. At the time of the Agrarian Land Reform he received a large plot but with low productivity, so it took a great amount of effort and the help of his children to successfully grow crops (mainly rice and some wheat.)

Today they have 8.5 hectares of Cabernet Sauvignon grapes planted.[/vc_column_text]

RED DEL VINO

SEGUNDO CASTRO GAETE

[vc_column_text]Segundo is a founding partner of Red del Vino (6th region) and he is the 4th of the Gaete brothers. His family suffered the loss of their father at an early age for which they had to work on the farm as children.

He left his studies at the age of 15 to work in the fields as he always preferred working to school. At 18 he began to work with his father on land that was given to them to grow rice and a few years later they purchased a plot.

On December 4, 1998, together with his brothers, he started the Sociedad Agrícola y Comercial Cal y Canto Ltda. Currently they produce Cabernet Sauvignon grapes and sell their wine under the label “Arado”[/vc_column_text]

GRAPES PLANTED

5.0 Ha Cabernet Sauvignon

RED DEL VINO

JUAN CASTRO GAETE

[vc_column_text]Juan is the last of the Castro Gaete brothers and is a founding partner of Red del Vino (6th region).

He studied at the Liceo Industrial de San Fernando graduating as Industrial Maintenance Technician.

He began by working in some mechanical workshops in the area, but with the help of his brothers he settled with his own factory, maintaining their stainless steel wine container tanks for the vineyards in the area, and then began to manufacture them.

Juan and his wife, Teresa have a daughter and son.

On December 4, 1998, together with his brothers, he started the Sociedad Agrícola y Comercial Cal y Canto Ltda. Currently they produce Cabernet Sauvignon grapes and sell their wine under the label “Arado” .[/vc_column_text]

GRAPES PLANTED

10 Ha Cabernet Sauvignon
3.5 Ha Carmenere

RED DEL VINO

BENITO CASTRO GAETE

[vc_column_text]Benito is a founding partner of Red del Vino (6th region) and one of the Gaete brothers. His family suffered the loss of their father at an early age for which they had to work on the farm as children.

Benito was always a good student however, he had to leave his higher studies due to financial problems. After a few years working in Santiago he returned to Peralillo and began to work together with his brothers in the vineyard.

Benito married and has a son and three daughters.

On December 4, 1998, together with his brothers, he started the Sociedad Agrícola y Comercial Cal y Canto Ltda. Benito is in charge of the administration and commercialization of the production of Cabernet Sauvignon grapes and their wine “Arado”[/vc_column_text]

RED DEL VINO

JOSÉ RAIMUNDO LÓPEZ

[vc_column_text]José is a founding partner of Red del Vino (6th Region). He moved to Cunaco and together with his wife, Eva, two sons and three daughters, he worked “the farm” which included corn and wheat. They were assigned 13.2 hectares as part of the Agrarian Land Reform process.

He devoted himself mainly to the cultivation of vegetable crops including potatoes, beans and corn which he knew well and then eventually producing blueberries. Later he planted a vineyard and dedicated himself to export table grapes for 20 years. Together with the neighbors, they planted additional vineyards, which they still operate today.

José grows and maintains the production of Chardonnay grapes.[/vc_column_text]

GRAPES PLANTED

13.2 Ha Chardonnay

RED DEL VINO

CARLOS LORCA SANDOVAL

[vc_column_text]

Carlos is a founding partner of the Red del Vino (6th region)

His parents were merchants and when he was 18, his father passed away, leaving him in charge of his family. As the oldest of seven siblings, he had to work hard to help his mother.

Later, he and his brother were able to purchase a remote plot of land so when the Agrarian Land Reform took place, they sold it and bought the rights of two other plots closer to their homes. They primarily planted corn and rice and then in 1998 began to plant vineyards due to the boom In the area.

Today he works with two of his brothers.

[/vc_column_text]

GRAPES PLANTED

15.5 Ha Cabernet Sauvignon

RED DEL VINO

OSVALDO DÍAZ POBLETE

[vc_column_text]Osvaldo is a founding partner of Red del Vino (6th region).

His parents together with his three sisters and brother settled in the Las Garzas Estate.

During the Agrarian Land Reform they lived in what was called “Settlement – Not one step back” on the same farm,. In the 1970’s his parents were awarded a plot of 13.2 hectares, planted with a half vineyard and a house that no longer exists.

His father planted the Merlot vine that today is recognized as Carmenere. He took cuttings from the same plant that existed in the assigned land.

When his father passed away, Osvaldo took over the plot, uprooting the entire vineyard that and leveling the land. His life has become the vineyard and the wine that is produced.

Osvaldo is an Electrical Technician by trade and he and his wife Marta have two daughters.[/vc_column_text]

GRAPES PLANTED

10.0 Ha Carmenere

ROSA SÁNCHEZ BRAVO

[vc_column_text]Rosa is a founding partner of Red del Vino (6th Region) and the only female partner in the organization. She also holds the position of Secretary. Rosa grew up with her 4 siblings in Santiago. Her mother was a merchant and father a mechanic. From young she loved nature and the outdoors. Rosa eventually moved away from Santiago to El Huape where she married her husband Don Hugo.

With the Agrarian Land Reform, the farm was organized as a community and her husband was elected president of the organization. After being awarded plots of land, they began to work with a diversity of crops, mainly vegetables. At the time of her husband’s death, they also had a tobacco plantation, which was not a successful business.

Despite the pressure Rosa received to sell her lands, she took charge and persevered to maintain her property. She personally planted part of the vineyards that today make up her heritage producing Cabernet Sauvignon and Carmenere grapes.[/vc_column_text]

GRAPES PLANTED

1.7 Ha Cabernet Sauvignon
2.4 Ha Carmenere

RED DEL VINO

GILBERTO LÓPEZ ARIAS

[vc_column_text]Gilberto is a founding partner of the Red del Vino (6th region). In 1977, he arrived to the area with his wife María and their eleven children.

During the Agrarian Land Reform, they worked as a community on the farm where they lived. The community was large and the farm was not able to provide for everyone so Gilberto applied for government benefits since their family was so large. They grew wheat, beans, potatoes and corn.

There was also a vineyard planted there but they had no experience growing vines so they had to adapt and learn to work the land. In 1978, they were able to produce their first harvest.

Today they produce Cabernet Sauvignon and Sauvignon Blanc grapes on 10.8 hectares.[/vc_column_text]

GRAPES PLANTED

10.5 Ha Cabernet Sauvignon

RED DEL VINO

JUAN AMADOR PÉREZ

[vc_column_text]At 13, Juan and his mother moved to a new employer where he began to work in the fields. Juan began driving tractors and eventually became the manager of the farm’s machinery. At 19, he married his wife Carmen and they have five daughters and a son.

With the Agrarian Land Reform, the land that he was working was transformed into a settlement, however after 3 years it was not prosperous. Juan moved his family to the Nancagua commune, where he continued to work in agriculture until the land was eligible for Agrarian Land Reform. He was awarded a plot with vineyards and a house. Juan did not have experience with vineyards so it was a new undertaking that he had to learn in order to succeed.

Today his land produces Cabernet Sauvignon and Carmenere grapes. Juan continues to reside in the same house and enjoys time with his 12 grandchildren and 8 great-grandchildren. He still works the field with his son-in-law.

.[/vc_column_text]

GRAPES PLANTED

3.0 Ha Cabernet Sauvignon
2.5 Ha Carmenere

RED DEL VINO

JAIME CEA MORALES

[vc_column_text]Jaime is a founding partner of the Red del Vino (6th region).

Born is Santiago, his family moved to Colchagua (the Las Garzas Estate) when he was 15 years old. Shortly after their arrival his stepfather died so he was in charge of taking care of his family.

At that time, the owner of the Las Garzas Estate, a house that was falling apart managed to get ahead with Jaime’s help and much hard work which included plowing 90 hectares on horseback and pulling vine shoots by hand.

Jaime married and they have seven children, one of whom currently works alongside him in the vineyard.

Proud of his work, he was assigned to the Agrarian Land Reform and spent five years in settlement before receiving his plot. He also became the leader of the first union that was formed in this sector.

His vineyard is his life which he loves passionately. He markets his wine under the name of “Puro Esfuerzo”, which is a Cabernet Sauvignon with strains and Carmenere.[/vc_column_text]

RED DEL VINO

GERARDO ORELLANA DÍAZ

[vc_column_text]Gerardo is a founding partner and President of the Red del Vino (6th region). In this role, he continues to work hard to grow and strengthen the organization.

From the age of 14 Gerardo fell in love with nature and activities in the field. He worked the farm and enjoyed taking the cows and calves to the pasture. The plot of land that his father received was partially planted with Sauvignon Blanc vines, however he focused more on traditional crop growing..

With his father’s passing in 1993, Gerardo took ownership of the land and in 1998 began planting more Sauvignon Blanc along with Chardonnay and Cabernet Sauvignon vines.[/vc_column_text]

GRAPES PLANTED

0.8 Ha Cabernet Sauvignon
2.5 Ha Chardonnay
6.5 Ha Sauvignon Blanc

RED DEL VINO

LUIS ORELLANA ALIAGA

[vc_column_text]Luis Enrique is a founding partner of Red del Vino (6th region). and he is also their Account Auditor.

Luis’ father received 13 hectares, of which 4.8 hectares were vineyards (Sauvignon Blanc) and the rest was land.

Luis left his studies to take charge of the vineyard when his father fell ill. As the eldest child, he assumed responsibility for the family and radically reorganized and adapted the plantation towards a more technical approach.

He currently produces Cabernet Sauvignon and Semillon grapes.

Married to Cecilia, they have three daughters.[/vc_column_text]

GRAPES PLANTED

7.0 Ha Cabernet Sauvignon
4.0 Ha Semillón

RED DEL VINO

DAGOBERTO MUÑOZ DÍAZ

[vc_column_text]Dagoberto is a founding partner of the Red del Vino (6th region).

At the age of 17 he arrived with his parents and three brothers at the El Carmen farm in the Chépica commune. He studied until second year of high school, leaving his studies to dedicate himself to working in the fields.

During the Agrarian Land Reform process, his employer voluntarily gave up the farm and lived in a settlement for 7 years with excellent results. When Dagoberto obtained his 12 hectare plot, for 20 years he dedicated himself to planting corn and having a cattle farm for consumption of meat and milk.

In the late 90’s he decided to plant vineyards. Which he maintains to this day, producing Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot grapes, he has been able to support his family with the fruit of his work in the fields, made up of four women and one man.[/vc_column_text]

GRAPES PLANTED

3.5 Ha Cabernet Sauvignon
0.5 Ha Merlot

RED DEL VINO

ÁNGEL CÁCERES

[vc_column_text]Ángel and José Luis are brothers and also founding partners of Red del Vino (6th region)

They live in the former San José del Carmen Estate which is a historic site and adds great value to their land.

Their father worked in property management and with time transitioned to work in the fields. At the time of the Agrarian Land Reform he received a large plot but with low productivity, so it took a great amount of effort and the help of his children to successfully grow crops (mainly rice and some wheat.)

Today they have 8.5 hectares of planted Cabernet Sauvignon grapes.[/vc_column_text]

GRAPES PLANTED

8.5 Ha Cabernet Sauvignon

ADRIÁN LORCA SANDOVAL

[vc_column_text]Adrián is a founding partner of the Red del Vino (6th region) .

His brother is Carlos Lorca Sandoval who is also part of the organization.

He and his Carlos were able to purchase a remote plot of land so when the Agrarian Land Reform took place, they sold it and bought the rights of two other
plots closer to their homes. They primarily planted corn and rice and then in 1998 began to plant vineyards due to the boom In the area.

Adrián produces Carmenere grapes.[/vc_column_text]

GRAPES PLANTED

14.5 Ha Carmenere

RED DEL VINO

ADÁN GÁLVEZ LEÓN

[vc_column_text]Adán is a founding partner of the Red del Vino (6th region). He was born in 1927 and grew up in the area. He married his wife, Maria after they met on the farm and they raised 7 children together.

During the Agrarian Land Reform process, they were assigned 6.8 hectares, which currently produces Syrah, Cabernet Sauvignon, Carmenere and Merlot. This land is where he began his relationship with the vineyards and he worked in this place as long as he was able to do so.

Due to his advanced age, he no longer participates directly in the field work, but he continues to contribute with his experience and knowledge and enjoy his retirement.[/vc_column_text]

GRAPES PLANTED

2.5 Ha Cabernet Sauvignon
1.5 Ha Carmenere
0.8 Ha Sirah
2.0 Ha Merlot

RED DEL VINO

ABELARDO BECERRA MENESES

[vc_column_text]José Abelardo is a founding partner and Treasurer of the Red del Vino (6th region) and has been an active member from the beginning of the organization.

Jose and his wife, Gloria have 4 children, two of which work with him on the farm. His childhood was difficult as his parents worked from dawn to dusk. Lack of resources made a formal education difficult for Jose who wanted a degree in Agriculture.

Eventually, Jose was able to acquire land through the Agrarian Land Reform process. He currently operates 9 hectares of vineyards, 6 of which are Cabernet grapes and three Semillón grapes.[/vc_column_text]

GRAPES PLANTED

6,0 Ha Cabernet Sauvignon
3,0 Ha Semillón
